items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Long time no see, Ragunna!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Mmm—mmm—! Can you smell that? Sweet o' Margherita's Restaurant. C'mon, c'mon, let's go stuff our faces already!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Let's take care of important matters first. You'll eat well soon, I promise.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Yeah, yeah, I know. We're here to find Cartethyia!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: I still can't believe Tethys couldn't unlock the Sentinel's Relic we found in Septimont...

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: At least now we know it's called the "Civilization Capsule." It probably holds key legacy data left behind by the Sentinel.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Like some ancient data recorder, right? But Tethys said to unlock it we'll need... the Leviathan's power.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: If it's really from the Sentinel, why do we need the Threnodian's power for that?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Maybe because they fused together at some point...

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Oh well, guess that's why we need to find Cartethyia. Maybe she'll know a workaround...

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: The Black Shores warned that the waters in Ragunna are acting weird again. Gotta be extra careful.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: And you know... something feels off in the city too. It's not as cheerful as when we left it.

options.text: I think things do feel more tense.
There is a sorrow in the air...

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Yeah, and they just threw a huge Carnevale not long ago too...

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Hmm... you might be onto something.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Uh... since when did you get all poetic?

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Amid howling winds, rising storms, and ghosts adrift, I heard the captain's call—

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Look! Over there! Isn't that Brant and Roccia? Wow, the Troupe of Fools is putting on a show!

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Hoist the sails, O humanity! The abyss and the summit have entwined. Your only path now is forward, bold and uncharted!

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: You stand on the precipice. Now is the hour to decide your fate—to plant the seed of your highest hope and watch it bloom into tomorrow.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Captain, why do you burn alone in your own flames?

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: If you do not first become ashes, how will you rebuild?

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Embrace your fire. Embrace your grief. Go into your solitude! I love those who dare to create, who strive to transcend, for in doing so, their old selves are destroyed. Yes, so they might reach the shores of a new life!

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: To journey onward is perilous. To stand still is perilous. To look back is also perilous. To tremble and falter... is perilous.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Now, I begin to understand what truly makes a hero great. It is to stand before humanity's deepest sorrow and its highest hope.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Oh, how I love those whose souls remain rich and vast, even when wounded. Even when the smallest risk could spell doom, they leap without hesitation into the unknown.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Should you stand alone, suffering in the endless dark... Today, may you hold fast to every last ember of courage and hope!

items.speaker: Onlookers
items.text: Bravo! Bravo!
Life... is but a walking shadow.
A poor player, who struts and frets his hour upon the stage... and then is heard no more.
It is a tale told by fools, full of sound and fury... Signifying nothing.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: {PlayerName}... {PlayerName}!

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: We could try this... "Nightmare Awakener"—

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: You're finally awake, {PlayerName}! One second I'm fine, the next my head's spinning and you're face-down on the ground...

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I'm fine. What I saw, though…

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Hmm... Sounds like the same thing happening to the people here.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: From what I've heard, many Ragunnesi have been having peculiar dreams lately. Even some of our Troupe members too.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: The details vary, but they all end the same way... the fall of Rinascita.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: And it's not just in dreamland either... {PlayerName}, I'm not sure if you've heard, but there have been strange rumors appearing across Rinascita lately.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Even in Septimont, folks claim they've seen Tacet Discords not of this land in the wilds, or the ghosts of their ancestors.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Thanks to all these oddities, the mood in Ragunna has gone sour.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: The Troupe performed today because Brant thought a new play to lighten the mood might give the people some hope.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: But here's the odd part. Only Rinascitans seem to be having these dreams. Visitors are spared... the same should be true of you.

options.text: Have any of you had similar dreams?
Have you seen other anomalies like this?
I'll get to the bottom of what's happening.

items.speaker: Roccia
items.text: Well... Not Brant or me. But someone in the Troupe has. They said they dreamed of sinking into an enormous, black ocean...

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: The Tacet Discords in the Stormy Sea have been more restless lately. And the "siren's" songs? Well, they've started sounding an awful lot like the Order's hymns...

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: We'll keep gathering intel on our end. Might be worth asking around the city to see who else has been experiencing this.

items.speaker: Brant
items.text: Rinascita's tides don't lie. Watch your back, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (What were those Tacet Discords whispering about? Gates of Paradise... and the word "Harbinger." Could they mean the "Harbinger of the Dark Tide"?)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (The only "Harbinger of the Dark Tide" is Fenrico. But he should still be sealing the Dark Tide at the Order's Fabricatorium...)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (That figure I saw... and Cartethyia's murder...)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (I came back to find her, so I should look for her first. She might know what's at the root of all this.)
